stem: A bookstore sells used paperbacks at a fixed price per book. Kenji buys several books and pays $\$5$ for every book he selects, plus a one-time $\$3$ membership fee. Which of the following functions gives the total amount $T$, in dollars, that Kenji pays for $n$ books?
explanation: The cost increases by $\$5$ for each book purchased, so the variable part is $5n$. The $\$3$ membership fee is a one-time fixed charge that does not depend on the number of books, so it is added as a constant: $T(n) = 5n + 3$. For example, buying 2 books costs $5(2) + 3 = 10 + 3 = \$13$, which matches paying $\$5$ per book plus the $\$3$ fee.

distractorRationale.A: Swaps the roles of the two numbers, using 3 as the per-book rate and 5 as the flat fee: $T(n) = 3n + 5$.
distractorRationale.C: Adds the per-book price and the membership fee together and uses their sum as a single per-book rate, treating the flat fee as if it also scales with each book: $T(n) = (5 + 3)n = 8n$.
distractorRationale.D: Ignores the one-time membership fee entirely and writes only the variable cost: $T(n) = 5n$.
